commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=326efdaec9e5bb0e32dd8b8a77f2e6d39b4431db)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a shared assistant-visible sanitizer matcher for `commentary to=functions.*`, routes Control UI assistant message extraction through that sanitizer, adds regression tests, and also doubles the memory-core dream narrative timeout.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +63. Total +71 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run in this read-only review. Source inspection and the linked screenshot issue give a clear focused path: `deltaText`-only WebChat stream events can bypass the changed message extractor and active stream rendering does not use the canonical assistant sanitizer.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action and maintainer review are needed because the PR needs real WebChat/gateway proof and still has code-scope blockers that should be fixed before merge.

target.title: fix(text): strip commentary to=functions.* trace lines from assistant output (fixes #89668)
target.body:
## What does this PR do?
Strips Anthropic-style `commentary to=functions.*` internal protocol lines from assistant output, preventing them from leaking into user-visible WebChat replies. Routes WebChat history/stream output through the canonical assistant-visible sanitizer.

## Related Issue
Fixes #89668

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking)

## Changes Made
- `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts`: Added `INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE` regex and integrated it into `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` to catch `commentary to=functions.browser` and similar internal protocol lines.
- `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts`: Changed `processMessageText` to use `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` (delivery profile) instead of `stripThinkingTags` (internal-scaffolding profile) for assistant messages, ensuring consistent sanitization across both `chat.history` and live streaming display paths.
- `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts`: Added focused regression test for `commentary to=functions.*` stripping.

## How to Test
1. Feed assistant text containing `commentary to=functions.browser ...` through the sanitizer
2. Verify the commentary line is stripped from output
3. Verify normal text is preserved

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:**
Internal tool-call commentary text (e.g. `commentary to=functions.browser`) was rendered into user-visible WebChat chat bubbles, making assistant replies look like they contained spam or broken output.

**Real environment tested:**
macOS 26.4.1, Node.js, openclaw upstream/main

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```bash
# 1. Test shared sanitizer
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts
# Result: 108 tests pass

# 2. Test message-extract sanitizer (WebChat display path)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts
# Result: 9 tests pass

# 3. Verify commentary pattern detection
node -e "const re=/^(?:>\\s*)?commentary\\s+to\\s*=\\s*[\\w.]+\\b/i; console.log('commentary to=functions.browser:', re.test('commentary to=functions.browser_tool_use:')); console.log('normal text:', re.test('Here is some normal text'));"
# Result: commentary to=functions.browser: true, normal text: false
```

**Evidence after fix:**
All 108 sanitizer tests pass. All 9 message-extract tests pass. Lines matching `commentary to=functions.*` pattern are now detected by `INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE` and stripped by `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` in all sanitizer profiles. Normal text remains unaffected.

**Observed result after fix:**
WebChat chat.history and live streaming output now go through the canonical `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` (delivery profile) instead of the weaker `stripThinkingTags` (internal-scaffolding profile), ensuring consistent stripping of internal commentary, tool-call XML, and other scaffolding.

**What was not tested:**
Live browser environment with actual OpenClaw gateway running.

## Checklist
- [x] Read Contributing Guide
- [x] Conventional Commits
- [x] Searched for duplicates
- [x] Only related changes
- [x] Tests pass
- [x] Added tests
- [x] Tested on macOS
